name: ptb-xl / 1_0_1 /
desc: PTB-XL v1.0.1 root — 21,837 ECG records, 3.2 GB total

children.name: ptbxl_database.csv
children.desc: Main metadata: 21,837 records × 28 columns (demographics, SCP codes, quality flags, fold assignments)

children.name: scp_statements.csv
children.desc: SCP-ECG diagnostic code reference table (71 codes, descriptions, diagnostic classes)

children.name: RECORDS
children.desc: Plain-text list of all 21,837 record paths (one per line)

children.name: example_physionet.py
children.desc: Official example script showing how to load records with the wfdb library

children.name: LICENSE.txt
children.desc: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)

children.name: SHA256SUMS.txt
children.desc: SHA-256 checksums for all files

children.name: records100 /
children.desc: 100 Hz ECG signals — 604 MB total; 24 subdirectories (1,000 records each)
Records with ecg_id 1–999 (example subdirectory)

children.name: 00001_lr.dat
children.desc: Binary WFDB signal file — 12 leads × 1,000 samples (10 s @ 100 Hz), 16-bit integers, 24 KB

children.name: 00001_lr.hea
children.desc: WFDB plain-text header — signal names, sampling rate, ADC gain, channel metadata

children.name: 00002_lr.dat
children.desc: Next record signal file (same format)

children.name: 00002_lr.hea
children.desc: Next record header file

children.name: 01000 / … 21000 /
children.desc: 23 further subdirectories with the same structure (1,000-record batches)

children.name: records500 /
children.desc: 500 Hz ECG signals — 2.6 GB total; same 24-subdirectory layout as records100
Records with ecg_id 1–999 (example subdirectory)

children.name: 00001_hr.dat
children.desc: Binary WFDB signal file — 12 leads × 5,000 samples (10 s @ 500 Hz), 16-bit integers, 120 KB

children.name: 00001_hr.hea
children.desc: WFDB plain-text header for the 500 Hz record

children.name: 00002_hr.dat
children.desc: Next record signal file (same format)

children.name: 00002_hr.hea
children.desc: Next record header file

children.name: 01000 / … 21000 /
children.desc: 23 further subdirectories with the same structure (1,000-record batches)
